boundary_reason: Concrete numeric thresholds make the proposal read tighter and more operational than a normal synthesis card, but the evidence still does not reach repeated bounded validation.
classification_rationale: This stays borderline only because proposal-level overvaluation pressure and genuine operational concreteness are both materially present at the same time.
why_not_likely_overvaluation: The proposal is unusually concrete, names numeric gates and is easier to operationalize than a normal synthesis card.
why_not_justified_exception: The card still lacks repeated bounded validation and remains a proposal rather than a proven local exception.
why_not_adjacent_classification: This is not a clear justified exception because proposal status and heuristic thresholds still matter. It is not a clear likely-overvaluation case because the card is unusually concrete and easier to operationalize than most proposal synthesis cards.
bounded_reading: Read as a proposal that may be defensible at the upper edge of S1 review, but still not as settled proof and not as evidence that new S2 axes are needed.

source_snapshot.overvaluation_signal: proposal confidence is already high and still lacks repeated bounded validation
source_snapshot.defensibility_signal: numeric gates make the proposal more operational and reviewable than a normal synthesis-only card
source_snapshot.review_anchor: Existing proposed_high_confidence review already marks numeric proposal cards as closer to acceptable than the strongest overvaluation cases.
